behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real `git` subprocess + parser handoff: rename detection can differ based on actual git output/flags, and a mock can hide a misconfiguration that reports rename as add+delete. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about git file-status categorization only, with no AI/ML decision-making or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert report has one rename entry and zero corresponding add/delete double-counting.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary-diff failures are typically encoding/subprocess-output issues that appear with real git behavior and real files. In-process stubs can miss crash conditions from actual command output and byte handling. Rejected higher levels: behavior concerns binary diff handling in git CLI context, not AI/ML output or agent pipelines.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, add/modify a binary file, run the CLI subprocess, assert exit success, no traceback on stderr, and binary file included in counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Unborn-branch/no-commit behavior depends on real git exit codes/messages from subprocess calls; mocks often normalize this and hide real failure handling. Rejected higher levels: behavior is CLI/git error-path handling only, with no model inference or multi-step agent loop.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess, assert successful execution and an empty structured report instead of an error.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against actual repository history, requiring real git history traversal and subprocess behavior; stubs can trivially mirror expected counts and miss real command/query mismatches. Rejected higher levels: text specifies git history counting, not AI/ML quality or chained agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run CLI subprocess, and compare report counts to independently computed expected counts from scripted git operations.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics rely on real git index/worktree behavior and command invocation details; in-process fakes can miss discrepancies in how the CLI queries git state. Rejected higher levels: behavior is git state equivalence, not AI/ML-dependent correctness or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, produce a set of changes, run CLI once with changes unstaged and once staged, and assert identical structured output for equivalent content changes.
